DECLARATION OF JOHN PAYNE IN SUPPORT OF DEFT CCSF'S OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM CLAIM REQUIRMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AIRPORT DIVISION
MINI-MINUTES FOR NOV-08-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER TONY NGUYEN'S AMENDED PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM CLAIM REQUIREMENT IS GRANTED. THE INVOICES SPECIFY PAYMENT WITHIN 30 DAYS OR THEREAFTER WITH INTEREST. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT DID NOT REFUSE TO PAY THE AMOUNT DUE UNTIL THE REJECTION LETTER OF APRIL 23, 2007 AS A CONSEQUENCE. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF MAY 15, 2007 WAS TIMELY. MOREOVER, DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT THROUGH REPEATED PROMISE TO PAY GIVE RISE TO THE DEFENSE OF ESTOPPEL TO ASSERT A STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PETITIONER TONY NGUYEN'S Amended Petition For Relief From Claim Requirement IS GRANTED. THE INVOICES SPECIFY PAYMENT WITHIN 30 DAYS OR THEREAFTER WITH INTEREST. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT DID NOT REFUSE TO PAY THE AMOUNT DUE UNTIL THE REJECTION LETTER OF APRIL 23, 2007 AS A CONSEQUENCE. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF MAY 15, 2007 WAS TIMELY. MOREOVER, DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT THROUGH REPEATED PROMISE TO PAY GIVE RISE TO THE DEFENSE OF ESTOPPEL TO ASSERT A STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Patrick J.
